name: API Deprecation Vocabulary
description: Controlled vocabulary for API deprecation, sunset, and end-of-life management.

terms.definition: Status indicating that an API surface remains operational but is no longer the preferred option and is scheduled for retirement.
A committed retirement date after which an API surface will no longer be available.
The point at which an API surface is no longer supported, served, or patched.
HTTP response header signaling that the requested resource is deprecated.
HTTP response header (RFC 8594) carrying an HTTP-date timestamp for resource retirement.
Link relation type 'sunset' connecting a resource to documentation about its retirement.
Replacement endpoint, version, or representation that consumers should migrate to.
Documented procedure consumers follow to move from the deprecated surface to its replacement.
Published rules describing notice periods, communication channels, and lifecycle stages for deprecation.
HTTP 410 status used to indicate that a previously available resource has been intentionally retired.
The act of removing an API surface from production after its sunset date.
Public communication declaring that an API surface is now deprecated.
Direct contact with identified consumers of a deprecated surface to confirm migration plans.
Time period between the deprecation announcement and the sunset date during which consumers must migrate.
Deprecation that applies to an entire API version rather than individual resources.
Deprecation scoped to a single endpoint, parameter, field, or schema property.
